topic.name: Federal Appropriations and Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal acts related to appropriations for specific programs and improvements in areas such as the arts, law enforcement, and veterans' healthcare.

Document 1: Amends the John F. Kennedy Center Act to allow the Trustees of the John F. Kennedy for the Performing Arts to study, plan, design, engineer, and construct a photovoltaic system
Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2012 to the Board for maintenance, repair, and security
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to increase by at least 50 for each of FY2008-FY2012 the number of full-time Deputy U.S. Marshals
Authorizes appropriations
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to waive the imposition or collection of copayments for telehealth and telemedicine visits of veterans
Authorizes appropriations

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on promoting health research, data collection, and public awareness for various medical conditions and treatments.

Document 1: Dental Health Promotion Act of 2009 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow reimbursement from flexible spending accounts for products used to diagnose, cure, mitigate, treat, or prevent the onset of tooth decay.
products used to diagnose, cure, mitigate, treat, or prevent the onset of tooth decay (caries), periodontal diseases, and conditions ailing the teeth, gums, and mouth
Document 2: National MS and Parkinson's Disease Registries Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop a system to collect data on multiple sclerosis (MS) and a system to collect data on Parkinson's disease.
establish a national surveillance system for the collection and storage of data for each such disease, including population-based registries of U.S. cases of MS and Parkinson's disease
Document 3: Uterine Fibroid Research and Education Act of 2007 - Requires the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to expand, intensify, and coordinate programs for the conduct and support of uterine fibroids research.
develop and disseminate to the public information regarding uterine fibroids, including information on: (1) the incidence and prevalence of uterine fibroids among women

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts that amend the Internal Revenue Code related to tax relief, investment, and financial planning.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase and extend through 2008 the alternative minimum tax (AMT) exemption amounts
Imposes tax penalties for underpayment of tax resulting from noncompliance with tax rules
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a corporation incorporated under the laws of Puerto Rico to elect to be treated as a domestic corporation for U.S. tax purposes
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow tax-free rollovers of amounts in qualified tuition programs to qualified ABLE programs

topic.name: Regulation of Biological Products
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating the sale and safety of biological products, including pet turtles and medical testing products.

Document 1: Prohibits the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) from restricting the sale by a turtle farmer or other commercial retail seller of a turtle that is less than 10.2 centimeters in diameter as a pet
the FDA has approved a plan submitted by the turtle farmer or seller relating to compliance with this Act
Document 2: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit a product labeled for research use only from being deemed adulterated or misbranded
engages in business communications with an end user regarding the product.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow a person to submit an application for licensure of a biological product based on its similarity to a licensed biological product
Prohibits a biological product from being evaluated against more than one reference product.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Responses to International Threats
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at addressing international threats, including drug trafficking, unresolved justice cases, and geopolitical conflicts.

Document 1: Directs the Attorney General to report to Congress on a national strategy to eliminate the illegal operations of National Threat Drug Gangs.
Sets forth criteria for the Attorney General to consider in identifying a National Threat Drug Gang.
Document 2: Urges the Secretary of State to express to Armenian officials the serious concern and strong desire of Congress to achieve justice in the unresolved murder of Joshua Haglund.
Calls on Armenia to continue pursuing, in cooperation with U.S. officials, the investigation into his death.
Document 3: Directs the President to submit a report to Congress, within 15 days after enactment of this Act, describing all known Syrian opposition groups.
Requires such report to include assessments of their composition and military, financial, and political capacities.

topic.name: Water Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on the safety and regulatory standards for drinking water and bottled water.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to identify and establish standards for contaminants in bottled water
Requires the Secretary to make such reports available to the public
Document 2: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require states exercising primary enforcement responsibility for public water systems to exempt any nonprofit small public water system
Precludes judicial or administrative review of such findings

topic.name: Nuclear Nonproliferation and Cooperation
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislation related to nuclear energy cooperation and nonproliferation efforts involving countries like India, Iran, and Syria.

Document 1: Approves the United States-India Agreement for Cooperation on Peaceful Uses of Nuclear Energy
U.S. policy to prevent the transfer to India of nuclear equipment, materials, or technology from other participating governments
Document 2: Directs the President...to submit such agreement to Congress
Prohibits the use of any Department of State funds...if the President fails to submit the agreement to Congress
Document 3: Maintains certain U.S. restrictions against the government of Syria...has dismantled biological, chemical, radiological, or nuclear weapons programs

Imposes specified export, financial, procurement, banking, and property sanctions on persons that transfer goods or technology contributing to Syria's biological, chemical, nuclear, or advanced conventional weapons programs

topic.name: Educational Improvement Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving student achievement and graduation rates in K-12 education through targeted support and accountability measures.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants to states for differentiated high school improvement systems targeting support to schools with low student achievement and graduation rates.
Requires state grantees to establish comprehensive school performance indicators and minimum annual improvement benchmarks.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to alter requirements for adequate yearly progress assessments of student groups.
Permits states to adopt alternative calculations of graduation and dropout rates to account for students who do not graduate on time.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to make matching grants to states to implement state middle school needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students matriculate to high schools with graduation rates below 60%.

topic.name: Health Insurance Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at regulating health insurance practices and ensuring consumer protection in the health insurance market.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to provide that the laws of the state designated by a health insurance issuer shall apply to individual health insurance coverage.
Prohibits an issuer from offering individual health insurance coverage in a secondary state if the state insurance commissioner does not use a risk-based capital formula.
Document 2: Amends the McCarran-Ferguson Act to provide that nothing in that Act shall modify, impair, or supersede the operation of any of the antitrust laws with respect to the business of health insurance.
Prohibits class action lawsuits on any claim against an insurer for a violation of the McCarran-Ferguson Act.
Document 3: Declares that federal provisions requiring the Secretary of Health and Human Services to review unreasonable premium increases shall not be construed to prohibit a state from imposing additional rate requirements.
Requires the Secretary to determine whether the state insurance commissioner or regulator will undertake corrective actions based on whether the state can adequately undertake such actions.

topic.name: Alternative Fuel Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the promotion, regulation, and taxation of alternative fuels and vehicles in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Clean Air Act to revise provisions concerning alternative fuel
prohibits the aftermarket conversion of a vehicle to alternative fuel operation from being considered tampering
Document 2: expands the definition of 'crude oil' for purposes of the excise tax on petroleum to include any bitumen or bituminous mixture
authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to include as crude oil any fuel feedstock or finished fuel product
Document 3: allow an excise tax credit through 2027 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas
requires 50% of all new vehicles purchased by the U.S. government to be capable of operating on compressed or liquefied natural gas

topic.name: Social Security and Veterans Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts related to modifications in Social Security benefits and compensation for veterans and their dependents.

Document 1: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act
Sets forth a schedule of additional benefit increases for such beneficiaries
Document 2: directs the Department of Veterans Affairs to increase, as of December 1, 2016, the rates of veterans' disability compensation
Each such increase shall be the same percentage as the increase in benefits provided under title II
Document 3: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act
to allow an employee... to elect irrevocably to have that employment treated as so covered

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the protection of personally identifiable information and establishing protocols for responding to data breaches.

Document 1: comprehensive identity protection coverage that is effective for the life of the individual
$5 million in identity theft insurance
Document 2: extends to state and federal regulatory officials having financial services oversight authority access to any information
without the loss of privilege or confidentiality protections
Document 3: criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach
Requires a person who owns or possesses data in electronic form containing a means of identification to provide prompt notice

topic.name: Courtroom Media Coverage
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations regarding the media coverage of court proceedings and the rights of participants in such proceedings.

Document 1: Requires the Supreme Court to permit television coverage of all open sessions of the Court
unless it decides by majority vote that allowing such coverage in a particular case would violate the due process rights of any of the parties involved.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to require the judge, at arraignment or at the commencement of a criminal trial, or at sentencing if there is no trial, to read in open court the rights of crime victims as provided by federal law.
Document 3: Sunshine in the Courtroom Act of 2013 - Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court (including the Supreme Court) or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings
except when such action would constitute a violation of the due process rights of any party.

topic.name: Tariff Suspensions on Specific Goods
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding temporary suspensions of duties on specific products.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on parts and accessories of optical instruments and apparatuses.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on bisphenol A Bis(3-methacryloyloxypropyl) ether substituted dimethacylate.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on yttrium oxides having a purity of at least 99.9 percent.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Health and Wellness for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving health and wellness services for individuals with disabilities, children, and mental health support in educational settings.

Document 1: Amends the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 to require standards for medical diagnostic equipment accessible to individuals with disabilities.
Establishes a National Advisory Committee on Wellness for Individuals With Disabilities.
Document 2: Establishes a pediatric specialty loan repayment program for physicians in shortage areas.
Determines shortage areas based on waiting times for patient care and recruitment for unfilled positions.
Document 3: Revises a community children and violence program to apply a public health approach to mental health services.
Requires comprehensive school mental health programs that are culturally and linguistically appropriate.

topic.name: National Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures related to national security, intelligence gathering, and voter registration processes.

Document 1: Amends the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to repeal on June 1, 2015, procedures outlined under the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978
Revises requirements for obtaining orders to prohibit disclosure of the receipt of a national security letter.
Document 2: Amends the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 to require states to designate federally-funded institutions of higher education as agencies for the registration of voters in federal elections.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General (AG) to establish minimization and destruction procedures governing the acquisition, retention, and dissemination of any records received by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
Defines 'minimization and destruction procedures' as specific procedures reasonably designed in light of the purpose and technique of a national security letter.

topic.name: Early Childhood Education Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving early childhood education and development programs.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to coordinate school improvement and professional development activities with early childhood development and education programs.
Requires states to create or revise early learning guidelines for preschool age children and early learning standards for children in kindergarten through grade three.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education and the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award competitive matching grants to states to support services for early childhood educators and early childhood care and education programs.
Requires grant applicants to demonstrate that they have a comprehensive early childhood educator professional development system or a plan for developing one.

topic.name: Mining Regulations and Land Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation of mining activities and the management of federal lands to protect environmental quality and historical sites.

Document 1: Withdraws federal lands within the Rainy River Drainage Basin in Minnesota from all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws

Requires an environmental impact statement conducted for such an action to include consideration of any potential impacts to units of the National Park System (NPS) or land in the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS) located in the Basin.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Bureau of Land Management (BLM), to carry out an expedited program to examine and determine the validity of each unpatented mining claim
Withdraws the mining townsites from all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws.

topic.name: Legislative Proposals and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative proposals and amendments related to federal laws and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Targeting Child Predators Act of 2016
amends the federal criminal code
Document 2: Establishes a program under which the Architect of the Capitol shall solicit
Requires the House Committee on House Administration to specify standards
Document 3: Agriculture Reauthorizations Act of 2015
reauthorizes and amends laws that address livestock mandatory price reporting requirements

topic.name: Human Rights and Legal Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at defining and protecting the rights of individuals, particularly relating to the sanctity of life and discrimination in health care practices.

Document 1: the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human
each human life begins with fertilization, cloning, or its functional equivalent
Document 2: prohibit the federal government and any state or local government from subjecting any health professional to discrimination
creates a cause of action for any violation of the abortion discrimination provisions
Document 3: 'marriage' includes a marriage, domestic partnership, civil union, or any other similar legal union
'spouse' refers to either member of such a legal union

topic.name: Labor Relations Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses recent legislative changes and proposals related to the National Labor Relations Act, focusing on collective bargaining, union organizing rights, and the powers of the National Labor Relations Board.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative (card-check)
Sets forth special procedural requirements for reaching an initial collective bargaining agreement following certification or recognition.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to repeal the authority of the General Counsel of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to issue, and prosecute before the Board, complaints with respect to unfair labor practices.
Revises Board powers to grant it the authority to investigate unfair labor practices, but repeals its power to prevent any person from engaging in them.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board, not earlier than seven days after it makes a final determination of the appropriate bargaining unit in a proposed election regarding collective bargaining representation, to acquire from the employer a list of all employees eligible to vote in the election.
Be made available to all parties, and include the employees' names as well as not more than one additional form of personal contact information.

topic.name: Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing public safety through stricter penalties and regulations related to crimes involving public officials and the exploitation of children.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to provide for a two-year increase in the prison sentence of public officials convicted of any crime
involving bribery, fraud, extortion, or theft of public funds greater than $10,000.
Document 2: prohibit financial transactions in interstate or foreign commerce that facilitate access to, or the possession of, child pornography
increase criminal penalties for sexual exploitation of children and for child pornography.
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term of up to 10 years for knowingly misrepresenting one's age in any Internet communication to a minor
to persuade, induce, entice, or coerce any individual to engage in illicit sexual activity.

topic.name: Legal Accountability and Penalties
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to criminal accountability, sentencing reforms, and the consequences of specific offenses for public officials.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the definition of 'aggravated felony' a third conviction for driving while intoxicated
Makes inadmissible or deportable an alien who has been convicted of driving while intoxicated
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to allow an exception to the minimum sentencing requirement for a crime of violence
for a law enforcement officer who committed the offense during or in relation to the pursuit of an individual
Document 3: Prohibits a Member of Congress from receiving an annuity or retired pay if such Member is convicted of bribery
Defines 'Member of Congress' as the Vice President, a member of the Senate or the House of Representatives

topic.name: Water Supply Projects
topic.description: Legislation focused on the planning, design, and implementation of water supply and management projects.

Document 1: Madera Water Supply Enhancement Project
Requires all planning, design, and construction of the Project to be undertaken in accordance with a cooperative agreement
Document 2: water rights compact between the Crow Tribe and the state of Montana
rehabilitate and improve the water diversion and delivery features of the Crow Irrigation Project
Document 3: feasibility of implementing a water supply and conservation project
improve water supply reliability in the Republican River Basin

topic.name: Meat and Poultry Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and amendments concerning the safety, traceability, and employment health standards in the meat and poultry industry.

Document 1: Meat and Poultry Products Traceability and Safety Act of 2007
establish a traceability system for all stages of production, processing, and distribution
Document 2: deem genetically-engineered fish to be unsafe under provisions related to new animal drugs
Document 3: require an active tuberculosis screening for any individual seeking employment with a poultry or meat processing facility

topic.name: Tax and Immigration Policies
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative measures related to taxation and immigration for nonresident individuals and entrepreneurs in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits a state from imposing an income tax on the compensation of a nonresident individual for any period in which such individual is not physically present in or working in such state.
such individual's work at home fails any convenience of the employer test or any similar test.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa (StartUp visa) for a sponsored alien entrepreneur.
whose commercial activities will generate required levels of employment, revenue, or capital investment.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce, in conducting the 2010 decennial census... to include in any questionnaire... a checkbox or similar option for respondents to indicate citizenship status or lawful presence in the United States.
Requires the Secretary to adjust census figures as necessary so that those who are not U.S. citizens or are not lawfully present in the United States are not counted in tabulating population.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at modernizing and improving the operations and delivery methods of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Amends provisions of federal law relating to the governance, workforce, and financing of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS).
Requires USPS to implement a program to provide a primary mode of mail delivery other than door delivery to residential and business addresses.
Document 2: Declares that it is the policy of the United States Postal Service (USPS) to provide access to secure, convenient package delivery receptacles.
Directs USPS to provide a primary mode of mail delivery other than door delivery, with a preference for secure, centralized delivery.

topic.name: LGBTQ+ Rights and Equality Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at promoting equality and protecting the rights of LGBTQ+ individuals in various sectors, including healthcare, military service, and public accommodations.

Document 1: status of the implementation of the National HIV/AIDS Strategy
international progress towards achieving universal access to HIV/AIDS treatment
Document 2: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to include sex, sexual orientation, and gender identity
Prohibits 'establishment' from being construed to be limited to a physical facility or place
Document 3: review the discharge characterization of any former members of the Armed Forces requesting a review who were discharged because of their sexual orientation
change a characterization to honorable if such characterization is any characterization except honorable

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Renewable Energy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management of federal lands, focusing on renewable energy projects, forest restoration, and habitat conservation.

Document 1: designate field or district offices of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Arizona, California, New Mexico, Nevada, Montana, and Wyoming to serve as Renewable Energy Pilot Project Offices
processing of oil and gas and renewable energy use authorizations on onshore federal land
Document 2: implement procedures to ensure that not less than 600,000 acres of federal land each fiscal year are treated
rehabilitation or restoration of such federal land, with highest priority given to land impacted by large-scale beetle infestations
Document 3: require proceeds from sales in the fourth area to be used for the implementation of a multispecies habitat conservation plan for the Virgin River
withdrawal and purchase authority with respect to the fifth and sixth areas

topic.name: Gulf Fisheries Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management and conservation of fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico, particularly red snapper.

Document 1: exclusive authority to manage and conserve the red snapper
fishery management plan for the sustainability of the red snapper
Document 2: amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
managing fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico
Document 3: require fishery management plans for overfished fisheries
evaluating progress to end overfishing and rebuild overfished stocks

topic.name: Legal Protections in Education and Justice
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing fairness, non-discrimination, and accountability within educational and judicial systems.

Document 1: Amends the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act to include expert witness fees
may be awarded to the prevailing party in a civil action
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code to require the Attorney General to report to Congress
protection of privileged and classified information contained in a report
Document 3: Prohibits public school students from being excluded from participating in, or subject to discrimination
Authorizes federal departments and agencies to enforce these prohibitions by cutting off the educational assistance

topic.name: Education Grants and Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal initiatives aimed at enhancing educational opportunities and pathways for students through grants and collaborative programs between educational institutions and organizations.

Document 1: establish a geography education grant program
enhance the geographic literacy of students in kindergarten through grade 12
Document 2: authorize matching six-year grants to local educational agencies
establish or support dual enrollment programs
Document 3: create and enhance educational and career pathways for high school students
award certain planning, implementation, transitional, and supplemental grants

topic.name: STEM and Workforce Education Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing STEM education and workforce development through grants, faculty recruitment, and program restructuring.

Document 1: expanding previously implemented reforms of undergraduate science, mathematics, engineering, or technology (STEM) education
implementing or expanding bridge, cohort, tutoring, or mentoring programs that enhance student recruitment or persistence
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to... programs of general dentistry or pediatric dentistry
encourage participating programs to use support to broadly recruit diverse faculty
Document 3: redesignate the Office of Vocational and Adult Education
make conforming amendments to the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006

topic.name: Air Travel Security Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on the rights and safety measures for air travelers during security screening processes.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from implementing any change to the prohibited items list of the Transportation Security Administration that would permit passengers to carry small, non-locking knives through passenger screening checkpoints.
Keep Knives Out of Our Skies Act
Document 2: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security to establish an air travelers' bill of rights for air passengers screened at primary airports.
Requires the air travelers' bill of rights to include, at a minimum, that a passenger has the right to presumed innocence before, during, and after screening.

topic.name: Manufactured Home Safety Standards
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety standards and environmental regulations for manufactured homes and consumer products.

Document 1: requires the consensus committee to develop and submit proposed federal manufactured home construction and safety standards
requires each manufactured home delivered for sale to be supplied with a weather radio
Document 2: Amends the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act
limit liability of a service station dealer
Document 3: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act of 2008
ban as a hazardous substance any children's cushioned product containing more than 1,000 parts per million flame retardant chemical

topic.name: Higher Education Financial Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating financial aid and funding for higher education institutions, particularly focusing on veterans' education and proprietary schools.

Document 1: Veterans Training Act - Deems a program of education as an approved program for purposes of the post-9/11 veterans' educational assistance program
the institution is an approved institution of higher learning
Document 2: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to determine a student's financial need and federal financial aid eligibility
requires ED to provide such tax return information to states and institutions of higher education
Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require proprietary institutions of higher education to derive at least 15% of their revenue from sources other than federal funds
limits what a proprietary institution may treat as revenue to the school in calculating whether it derives at least 15% of its revenue from non-federal funds

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster addresses various aspects of U.S. immigration policy, including enforcement procedures, customs services, and legal protections for children in immigration contexts.

Document 1: prohibiting the construction of border tunnels
federal criminal code
Document 2: authorize the Commissioner of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection
provide additional customs and immigration services
Document 3: apprehension procedures for immigration enforcement
protect the best interests of children of detained individuals

topic.name: U.S.-Cuba Relations Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modifying U.S. restrictions and regulations related to trade, travel, and diplomatic relations with Cuba.

Document 1: Waives certain foreign assistance and trade and travel prohibitions against Cuba
Prohibits the President from denying visas to such nationals
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo placed upon all trade with Cuba
Permits travel to and from Cuba by U.S. citizens or residents
Document 3: Prohibits that President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by any U.S. person
Directs the President to rescind all regulations that so regulate or prohibit such travel

topic.name: Military Operations and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations related to military operations, including the use of munitions, unmanned aerial vehicles, and funding for military construction.

Document 1: Places specified restrictions on the use of cluster munitions.
Directs the President to submit a cleanup plan for unexploded munitions.
Document 2: Prohibits the use of unmanned aerial vehicles for lethal actions by CIA personnel.
Requires the President to transfer UAV strike authority to the Department of Defense.
Document 3: Appropriates funds for military construction for various branches of the military.
Specifies restrictions and authorizations regarding the use of appropriated funds.

topic.name: Energy Market Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and taxation of energy markets, including oil and hydroelectric power.

Document 1: impose an 0.01% excise tax on transactions in oil futures, options, and swaps
require withholding of such tax if the buyer is a foreign person
Document 2: modify certain statutory schedules governing contracts for delivery of hydroelectric power generated at Hoover Dam
allocate through the Western Area Power Administration (WAPA) for delivery

Document 3: repeal provisions amending the Natural Gas Act to extend its jurisdiction
Natural Gas Act shall be applied and administered as if such provisions and attendant amendments had not been enacted

topic.name: Government Accountability and Ethics
topic.description: This cluster discusses reforms aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and ethical standards within the federal government and its institutions.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to revise post-employment restrictions on former officers, employees, and elected officials
Grants subpoena power to the OCE board to carry out its duties.
Document 2: Requires the President to appoint, with the advice and consent of the Senate, one additional district judge for the district of Nebraska.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to reduce from 14 years to 7 years the term of office of the members of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
Increases from two years to four years the post-service restriction which renders Board members ineligible to hold any office, position, or employment in any member bank.

topic.name: Support for Veteran Farmers
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at providing assistance and resources to military veterans pursuing agricultural careers.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Risk Protection Act regarding the value-added agricultural product market development grants program to include farmers and ranchers who are veterans.
Establishes the position of Military Veterans Agricultural Liaison to provide returning veterans with information about agricultural programs.
Document 2: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to extend the beginning farmer and rancher development program, providing assistance for agricultural rehabilitation and vocational training for veterans.
Permits conservation reserve contract modification to facilitate transfers from retiring owners to limited resource farmers or ranchers.

topic.name: Maritime Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to the management and regulation of maritime activities and environmental protections in navigable waters.

Document 1: Expands the boundaries of the Thunder Bay National Marine Sanctuary and Underwater Preserve in Lake Huron.
Document 2: Directs the U.S. Coast Guard to develop a land-based system to backup its Global Position System (GPS).
Revises the deadline for fishing vessels' compliance with safety programs.
Document 3: Establishes uniform national standards for discharges incidental to vessel operation into navigable waters.
Requires the Secretary to issue a rule for ballast water discharges.

topic.name: Federal Budget Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at modifying appropriations, budgetary controls, and spending regulations within the federal budget framework.

Document 1: This bill amends the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
exempt Indian health facilities and services from sequestration.
Document 2: This bill requires that if the total amount appropriated for the Executive Office for Immigration Review does not exceed $485.3 million
the total amount authorized to be appropriated for FY2018 and each of the three succeeding fiscal years.

topic.name: Government Research and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal initiatives aimed at improving systems and processes through research, evaluation, and oversight across various sectors including energy, child welfare, and information classification.

Document 1: research the future resilience and reliability of the nation's electric power transmission and distribution system
SMART Study
Document 2: carry out a pilot program of grants to child welfare agencies
research, through grant or contract, the successes and challenges of the programs established under the pilot program grants
Document 3: require each agency inspector general to carry out not fewer than three evaluations
require the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to publish, annually, information on the status of agency efforts at reducing the holding of classified information

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating contributions and expenditures in federal elections to ensure transparency and limit foreign influence.

Document 1: ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals
increases the civil monetary penalties applicable to foreign nationals
Document 2: eliminate the limitations on direct contributions to candidates
require 24-hour notification for direct contributions
Document 3: designate an individual for each of a candidate's authorized campaign committees
procedures for filing and revoking such a designation

topic.name: Federal Research and Development Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses various federal initiatives aimed at improving forecasting, sustainable chemistry, and remote sensing through research and collaboration among government agencies and educational institutions.

Document 1: The Department of Commerce, through the National Weather Service (NWS) and other appropriate programs in the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), shall: collect and utilize information in order to make usable, reliable, and timely forecasts of seasonal temperature and precipitation in agriculture.
Commerce shall build upon existing forecasting and assessment programs and partnerships.
Document 2: Directs the President to establish an interagency Sustainable Chemistry Program to promote and coordinate federal sustainable chemistry research, development, demonstration, technology transfer, commercialization, education, and training activities.
Requires the Interagency Working Group to submit a report to Congress, as well as to the Government Accountability Office.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the United States Geological Survey (USGS), to: establish and maintain a nationwide AmericaView Program to advance the availability, distribution, and use of remote sensing data.
award annual grants to sustain and develop StateView programs.

topic.name: Federal Agency Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations governing the operations and financial practices of federal agencies, including infrastructure for alternative fuels and the management of collected fines and fees.

Document 1: allows federal agencies to construct, operate, and maintain alternative fuel infrastructure
must charge fees for alternative fuel provided that are sufficient to cover the costs
Document 2: requires the Nuclear Regulatory Commission to retain amounts collected for safety-related fines
distribute them to counties for maintaining radiological emergency preparedness plans

topic.name: Emergency Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving preparedness and response to emergencies, particularly for vulnerable populations and education continuity.

Document 1: require the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to provide guidance to implement plans for outreach to people with disabilities during a terrorist or other incident.
Document 2: requires the Director of the Terrorist Screening Center to review the completeness of the Terrorist Screening Database and the terrorist watch list utilized by the Transportation Security Administration (TSA)
to determine if an individual poses a threat to aviation or national security.
Document 3: authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to states for assistance in restarting school operations in major disaster areas
provides temporary emergency impact aid to states and LEAs for the education of students displaced by a major disaster.

topic.name: Bankruptcy and Debt Collection Law
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to bankruptcy proceedings and debt collection practices, focusing on consumer protections and the treatment of debts in bankruptcy cases.

Document 1: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor
Permits a Chapter 13 bankruptcy plan to modify the rights of claim holders
Document 2: Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act to prohibit a debt collector from bringing, or threatening to bring, legal action against any consumer on a debt in which the statute of limitations has expired
Requires a debt collector to disclose to the consumer that the debt has been transferred to the debt collector
Document 3: Amends federal bankruptcy law to declare nondischargeable in bankruptcy any debts for a personal injury that results in a permanent disability

topic.name: Tribal Governance and Protection Laws
topic.description: Legislation focused on the governance, cultural preservation, and legal protections for Native American tribes and their resources.

Document 1: prohibits any Indian tribe or a tribal organization from cultivating, manufacturing, or distributing marijuana on Indian lands
No funds authorized or appropriated by federal law shall be made available to an Indian tribe or a tribal organization determined to have violated this Act
Document 2: increases the maximum term of imprisonment from 5 years to 10 years for individuals convicted more than once of illegal trafficking in Native American human remains or cultural items
bans the export of illegally obtained Native American cultural objects and sets penalties for violations of this ban
Document 3: Amends the Indian Self-determination and Education Assistance Act to revise tribal self-governance requirements
authorizes the Secretary of the Department of the Interior to select up to 50 new Indian tribes per year from those eligible to participate in self-governance

topic.name: Employee Compensation and Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to employee compensation, benefits, and workplace rights.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act of 2000
a claim that an individual qualifies for compensation or benefits under such Act shall be considered
Document 2: increase the amount of wages eligible for the work opportunity tax credit for veterans
tax-exempt organizations to claim the lesser of the amount of the work opportunity tax credit for hiring veterans
Document 3: require each employer who employs 100 or more employees to provide each employee one week of paid vacation
the Secretary of Labor to conduct a public awareness campaign, through the Internet and other media, to inform the public of the entitlement to paid leave under the Act

topic.name: Healthcare Legislation and Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving healthcare access and addressing mental health services within various programs, including those for veterans and military personnel.

Document 1: Obamacare Full Disclosure Act
cancellation notice to include specified information under specified circumstances
Document 2: Amends the TRICARE program
authorize the provision of mental health counseling for TRICARE participants
Document 3: Veterans Access to Care Act
designate medical facilities of the Department of Veterans Affairs as health professional shortage areas

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization Acts
topic.description: Legislation authorizing appropriations for U.S. intelligence and related activities across various government agencies.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2011, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 2: This bill authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
The amounts authorized, and the authorized personnel ceilings, are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2007 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2007, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.

topic.name: Credit Reporting Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving consumer protection and transparency in credit reporting processes.

Document 1: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act with respect to an item of adverse information about an active duty military consumer
requires a consumer reporting agency to notify promptly an active duty military consumer whenever it receives an item of adverse information about him or her
Document 2: requires states to carry out congressional redistricting in accordance with a process under which members of the public are informed of redistricting proposals via the Internet
requires each state redistricting entity to establish and maintain a public Internet site meeting specified requirements
Document 3: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act with respect to the procedures followed by consumer reporting agencies when a consumer disputes the completeness or accuracy of information
requires consumer reporting agencies to expand the dispute notification furnished to any person who provided disputed information to include all documentation provided by the consumer

topic.name: Wilderness and Land Protection Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at designating, managing, and protecting wilderness areas and tribal lands in Washington state.

Document 1: Designates certain land in the Mount Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest as wilderness.
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate the Middle Fork Snoqualmie and Pratt Rivers as scenic and/or wild rivers.
Document 2: Removes certain federal land within Olympic National Park from inclusion in the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Takes specified federal land within the Park into trust for the Quileute Indian Tribe.
Document 3: Identifies roadless areas within the National Forest System as National Forest Inventoried Roadless Areas.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to manage such Areas to maintain their roadless character.

topic.name: Predatory Lending and Foreclosure Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating mortgage lending practices and preventing foreclosure through consumer protection and assistance programs.

Document 1: Prohibit any person... from providing mortgage lending services unless such person is certified
Authorize the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund to make grants to nonprofit community development corporations to educate and train borrowers
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the compensation of fee appraisers
Document 3: Prohibits a covered mortgagee from initiating or continuing a foreclosure proceeding... unless: (1) the mortgagee has determined whether the mortgagor is eligible for a qualified loan modification
Directs the Secretary to make competitive grants to state and local governments to establish compulsory mortgagee-mortgagor mediation programs

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing Medicare coverage, transparency, and cost management for beneficiaries.

Document 1: Directs the Comptroller General to review and report to Congress on the retail cost of prescription drugs in the United States
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to include in the comparative plan information for beneficiaries under new Medicare part D
Document 2: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid to report annually on Medicare Advantage enrollment data.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to limit increases in the monthly Medicare premium
including the Medicare Advantage prescription drug program premium, to 25% of any Social Security cost of living increase.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers from unfair practices in various markets, including automotive advertising, rent-to-own transactions, and internet sales tax regulations.

Document 1: Requires any advertisement for any model of automobile or light duty truck to include the Environmental Protection Agency's Combined Miles Per Gallon Rating
Treats a violation as an unfair or deceptive act or practice under the Federal Trade Commission Act
Document 2: Prohibit a seller in a rent-to-own transaction from receiving or assessing any interest, finance charge, or other fee for the transaction that exceeds the interest, fees, or finance charges permissible under the laws of the state
Sets forth required disclosures, prohibitions, and enforcement, as well as civil liability for failure to properly disclose the terms of a rent-to-own transaction
Document 3: Authorizes each member state under the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement to require all sellers not qualifying for a small-seller exception to collect and remit sales and use taxes
Amends the Internet Tax Freedom Act to extend until November 1, 2024: the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access

topic.name: Retirement and Employee Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments aimed at enhancing retirement security and employee benefits for workers and retirees.

Document 1: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to require any civil action brought by a beneficiary or participant of an employee benefit plan to be adjudicated as a de novo proceeding.
Equality for Workers under ERISA Act of 2007
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income distributions from individual retirement plans for payments on the mortgage of a taxpayer's principal residence.
Homeowner Empowerment Act of 2008
Document 3: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to add a Personal Social Security Savings Program.
Securing Medicare and Retirement for Tomorrow Act of 2007, or SMART Act of 2007

topic.name: Financial Regulation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at regulating and overseeing financial institutions and systemic risks within the U.S. financial system.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to apply the bank share or asset acquisition requirements of the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956
Subjects an industrial bank to Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) requirements regarding registration, reporting, and bank examinations.
Document 2: Establishes the Financial Services Systemic Risk Oversight Council
Requires the Council to regulate systemic risk in the U.S. financial system.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Immigration Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the welfare of children, particularly in the context of immigration and child care services.

Document 1: Unaccompanied Alien Children Placement Transparency Act of 2016
require the Department of Health and Human Services or the Department of Homeland Security to provide information to the governor and each appropriate agency of a state
Document 2: Quality Child Care for America Act
authorize appropriations for child care workforce development initiatives
Document 3: Child Welfare Provider Inclusion Act of 2014
prohibits the federal government, and any state that receives federal funding for child welfare services from discriminating against a child welfare service provider

topic.name: Federal Property Management and Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conveyance, and use of federal properties for various public and cultural purposes.

Document 1: Authorizes the head of each executive department to convey real and related property under the department's ownership and control
Directs the head of each of the Departments of Defense (DOD), Agriculture, and Energy (DOE) to develop and carry out a plan to enter into agreements with a nongovernmental person
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to convey, by quitclaim deed, to the National Women's History Museum, Inc. specified property in the District of Columbia
Requires the property to be dedicated for use as a site for a national women's history museum for a 99-year period
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers who create literary, musical, artistic, scholarly compositions, or similar property a fair market value tax deduction for contributions
The bill limits the amount of the deduction based upon the donor's artistic adjusted gross income

topic.name: Animal and Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management and protection of animal welfare and public health in relation to emergency medical responses and equine integrity.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act, with respect to asthma-related grants for child health services
requires trained personnel to administer epinephrine to a student
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to enter into an agreement with the Corolla Wild Horse Fund
provide for the management of free-roaming wild horses
Document 3: Requires an independent anti-doping organization with responsibility for ensuring the integrity and safety of horse races
developing, publishing, and maintaining rules regarding substances, methods, and treatments that may and may not be administered to a horse

topic.name: Trade Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. trade legislation aimed at enhancing economic competitiveness and addressing international obligations.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to provide for the duty-free treatment of certain recreational performance outerwear.
Establishes the Sustainable Textile and Apparel Research Fund (STAR Fund).
Document 2: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to authorize the President to designate certain luggage and travel articles as eligible for duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP).
Document 3: Declares that it is the purpose of this Act to attain the immediate return of Sean Goldman and all children of the United States who are being held wrongfully in Brazil.
Directs the President to notify World Trade Organization (WTO) member countries... that the United States is suspending the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) for Brazil.

topic.name: Interagency Coordination Strategies
topic.description: This topic encompasses strategies and policies aimed at enhancing interagency collaboration and resource management in national security, energy, and food security sectors.

Document 1: Establishes a Committee on National Security Personnel within the Executive Office of the President.
Requires such Committee to issue a National Security Human Capital Strategy to develop the national security and homeland security personnel.
Document 2: Establishes the Commission on Comprehensive Strategies for the Placement of Natural Gas Infrastructure.
Conduct a comprehensive review of U.S. natural gas policy to identify problems and make recommendations.
Document 3: States the policy objective of promoting global food and nutrition security.
Requires the Administrator of USAID to coordinate the development and implementation of a Feed the Future Strategy.

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating specific behaviors and practices in society, focusing on issues of safety, protection, and privacy.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to make it unlawful for anyone whose federal license to import, manufacture, or deal in firearms has been revoked...
Imposes a fine and/or prison term of not more than one year for violations of this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to list permissible sources of dogs and cats used by research facilities...
Sets forth additional monetary penalties for related violations.
Document 3: This bill amends the federal criminal code to make it unlawful to knowingly distribute a photograph, film, or video of a person engaging in sexually explicit conduct...
The bill establishes exceptions to the ban.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Native Tribes
topic.description: The documents discuss the process and implications of extending federal recognition to various Native American tribes, including eligibility for federal services and the establishment of governance structures.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Muscogee Nation of Florida
Makes the Nation and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits furnished to federally recognized Indian tribes.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation of Fossil Fuels
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating greenhouse gas emissions and pollutants from fossil fuel energy production, particularly focusing on coal and natural gas.

Document 1: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from issuing, implementing, or enforcing any proposed or final rule under the Clean Air Act that establishes a performance standard for greenhouse gas emissions from any new source that is a fossil fuel-fired electric utility generating unit.
Requires the Administrator to separate sources fueled with coal and natural gas into separate categories.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct studies of the risk of carbon leakage and changes in U.S. industrial output and investment resulting from the implementation of a cap-and-trade program.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to require affected units to reduce mercury emissions by the less stringent limitation of: (1) 80% capture of inlet mercury or an emission rate of 1.6 pounds of mercury per trillion British thermal units (Btu) of input coal for the period beginning on January 1, 2012, and ending December 31, 2014.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Trade Policies
topic.description: These documents discuss various legislative amendments related to environmental regulations and trade policies affecting specific countries and regions.

doc_id_1: Amends the Department of the Interior
doc_id_2: Withdraws the extension of nondiscriminatory treatment
doc_id_3: National Forest System land in Alaska

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Transparency
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures aimed at enhancing transparency and public involvement in federal regulatory processes and decision-making.

Document 1: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise the process by which Medicare administrative contractors (MACs) issue and reconsider local coverage determinations (LCDs)
provide specified information related to whether the determination failed to correctly apply qualifying relevant evidence
Document 2: requires each federal agency to submit a monthly report to the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (OIRA)
OIRA must make such monthly reports publicly available on the Internet
Document 3: improve the quality of the deliberations of Congress with respect to proposed federal mandates by providing Congress and the public with more complete information
ensure that Congress acts on such mandates only after focused deliberation on their effects

topic.name: Student Loan and Agricultural Loan Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing borrower protections and modifying loan guarantee programs for education and agriculture sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require private educational lenders to disclose to borrowers certain information concerning the terms of the loan.
Requires private educational lenders to disclose to borrowers who become 30 days delinquent on their loan the date on which the loan will be charged-off.
Document 2: This bill amends the Agricultural Act of 1961 to revise the limits for the Department of Agriculture's conservation loan guarantee program.
USDA may not guarantee a loan under the program that exceeds $3.5 million.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private educational loans for which cosigners are held jointly liable to describe clearly and conspicuously the cosigners' obligations.
Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to publish a model form for describing those obligations.

topic.name: U.S. Territories Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the economic and energy policies affecting U.S. territories and freely associated states.

Document 1: annual report on the gross domestic product of U.S. territories
revisions concerning the Guam and Northern Mariana Islands Visa Waiver Program
Document 2: amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005
loan guarantees for ocean thermal energy conversion systems projects in U.S. territories

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Control
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating the manufacture, distribution, and safety of controlled substances to prevent abuse and protect public health.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prescribe new drug application requirements for tamper-resistant drugs
Requires the Secretary to refuse a new drug application for any new drug containing opium, an opiate, or a derivative as an active moiety that is not tamper-resistant.
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to make it unlawful for any adult to knowingly and intentionally manufacture, create, distribute, or dispense a Schedule I or II controlled substance combined with a candy product
Imposes enhanced criminal penalties and a one-year minimum sentence for violations.
Document 3: Amends the Controlled Substances Import and Export Act to prohibit the manufacture or distribution of a controlled substance in schedule I or II
Prohibits the manufacture or distribution of a listed chemical intending or knowing that it will be used to manufacture a controlled substance.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Health Care
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to veterans' disability compensation, educational benefits, and medical readiness for military service members.

Document 1: Adds Parkinson's disease to the list of diseases presumed to have been incurred in or aggravated by military service
compensable under veterans' disability compensation
Document 2: Allows former members of the Armed Forces to transfer educational benefits to family members
entitled to Post-9/11 Educational Assistance
Document 3: Revises the effective date for being considered on active duty
entitlement to active-duty medical and dental care for members of the reserves

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Reform
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at amending immigration laws to address issues related to high-skilled immigrants, family reunification, and visa categories.

Document 1: eliminate the per country numerical limitation for employment-based immigrants
increase the per country numerical limitation for family based immigrants
Document 2: revise waiver of inadmissibility requirements
authorize parents of U.S. citizens to apply for a waiver of inadmissibility
Document 3: create an E-4 treaty trader visa category for nationals of South Korea
perform specialty occupation services

topic.name: Affordable Housing Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at promoting affordable housing and employment opportunities for low- and very low-income individuals.

Document 1: requires that at least 20% of employee hours be performed by low- or very low-income persons
establish a section 3 committee of interested parties to oversee compliance
Document 2: allow a student who is a homeless child or youth or a student who is a homeless veteran to occupy low-income housing units
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to convert such contract to a section 8 project-based assistance contract
preserving troubled projects facing foreclosure

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving health care services and support for veterans in various capacities.

Document 1: provides access to magnetic EEG/EKG-guided resonance therapy to treat veterans suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder, traumatic brain injury, military sexual trauma, chronic pain, or opiate addiction
requires the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to carry out a one-year pilot program
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to make interactive training sessions available for family caregivers
provides family caregivers with information concerning public, private, and nonprofit agencies that offer support
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to determine whether the needs of veterans in southern New Jersey for full-service medical care shall be met
construction of a new full-service, Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical center to provide inpatient and outpatient services

topic.name: Federal Grant Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss various federal legislative acts that authorize grant programs aimed at addressing specific community needs, including education, mental health, and environmental conservation.

Document 1: continues the Medicaid emergency psychiatric demonstration project
authorizes the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services to replace with another state
Document 2: authorizes the Secretary of Education to award five-year grants
provides expanded school choice opportunities to students
Document 3: directs the Secretary of the Interior to enter into cooperative agreements
administer grants for recreation, historic, archeological, and cultural preservation

topic.name: Energy Efficiency and Sustainability in Government Projects
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at enhancing energy efficiency and sustainability in military and energy sector construction and technology development.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Defense to incorporate design criteria from the Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design Green Building Rating System.
Requires military construction renewable energy goals to include specific goals to increase the use of renewable energy sources.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy to carry out a research, development, and technology demonstration program to improve the efficiency of gas turbines.
Sets forth as program goals to achieve advanced high efficiency gas turbines that can achieve at least 65% combined cycle efficiency.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions and providing support to businesses in the context of economic stability.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of an increase in the guarantee fee required to be charged by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury from selling any outstanding shares of senior preferred stock until Congress passes specific legislation.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to repeal the authority of the Board of Governors to discount notes for private entities in unusual circumstances.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the President should encourage financial institutions to maintain historic levels of lending to small and medium-sized businesses.
Establishes a revolving loan fund to provide temporary loans to businesses with less than 1,000 full-time equivalent employees.

topic.name: Naming Designations of Facilities
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures that designate specific names for community facilities, such as clinics and post offices, within the United States.

Document 1: Designates the community based outpatient clinic of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) located at 310 Home Boulevard in Galesburg, Illinois, as the 'Lane A. Evans VA Community Based Outpatient Clinic.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2523 7th Avenue East in North Saint Paul, Minnesota, as the 'Mayor William 'Bill' Sandberg Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 220 East Oak Street, Glenwood City, Wisconsin, as the 'Second Lt. Ellen Ainsworth Memorial Post Office.'

topic.name: Government Oversight Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing oversight and accountability within federal agencies and boards.

Document 1: Amends the Public Interest Declassification Act of 2000
extend Board authority through 2014.
Document 2: Amends the Inspector General Act of 1978
Requires the heads of those entities to take action to address deficiencies identified by their Inspector General.
Document 3: Prohibits any federal agency or department from establishing or implementing an internal policy
discourages or prohibits the selection of a location that is perceived to be a resort or vacation destination.

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at altering tax policies related to specific industries and initiatives.

Document 1: Ending Subsidies for Big Oil Act of 2007
deny a tax deduction for income attributable to the sale, exchange, or other disposition of oil, natural gas, or any primary product thereof.
Document 2: Tanning Tax Repeal Act of 2014
repeal the 10% excise tax on indoor tanning services.
Document 3: Historic Homeownership Revitalization Act of 2013
allow a new tax credit for 20% of rehabilitation expenditures for a historic home used as a principal residence.

topic.name: Energy Efficiency and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing energy efficiency, promoting renewable energy technologies, and supporting energy conservation initiatives.

Document 1: amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to reauthorize through FY2025 the program of hydroelectric production incentives
incentive payments to the owners or operators of hydroelectric facilities at existing dams to make capital improvements
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a pilot program to award grants and loan guarantees to no more than six hospitals
significantly improving energy efficiency and encouraging on-site power generation
Document 3: Authorizes federal acquisition of, or the provision of federal funds to states for purchase of, only green technologies
renewable energy and energy efficiency products and services that reduce dependence on unreliable sources of energy

topic.name: Medicare Policy Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes and provisions related to Medicare services and provider regulations.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
extend through July 1, 2012, Medicare incentive payments for physician scarcity areas
Document 2: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
allow an authorized physician or practitioner who is not enrolled as a Medicare provider to prescribe covered drugs under the Medicare prescription drug benefit
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
with respect to the prospective payment system (PPS) for home health services

topic.name: Rail Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations aimed at improving safety and operational standards for rail transportation systems.

Document 1: Requires each rail carrier that is a Class I railroad, that has inadequate safety performance, or that provides intercity passenger or commuter rail passenger transportation to develop and submit for Secretary of Transportation approval a plan to implement a positive train control system that prevents rail collisions.
Increases penalties for violations of rail safety regulations or orders issued by the Secretary.
Document 2: Grants state, county, and municipal governments, as well as multi-jurisdictional transportation authorities that operate or manage roads, bridges, railroads, buses, ferries, or other transportation systems, rulemaking authority to establish toll, user fee, or fare discount programs for their local residents or commuters.
Document 3: Requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan, meeting specified requirements and approved by the Surface Transportation Board, for any of its rail cars that it stores for three or more years.

topic.name: Economic Development and Local Governance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies aimed at promoting economic development in specific local areas, particularly through the designation of metropolitan statistical areas and support for underutilized business zones.

Document 1: Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to consider Brunswick County, North Carolina, to be part of the metropolitan statistical area that contains Wilmington, North Carolina.
Document 2: Amends Small Business Act provisions relating to HUBZones (historically underutilized business zones) to define 'qualified suburban area' as any village, city, town, or economic development area governed by a public authority.
Document 3: Amends the Secure Rural Schools and Community Self-Determination Act of 2000 to extend the Act through FY2013.

topic.name: Environmental Management and Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures aimed at improving environmental management and infrastructure resilience in response to ecological threats and natural disasters.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to lead a multiagency effort to slow the spread of Asian Carp
support to state and local government strategies, to slow, and eventually eliminate, the threat posed by such carp.
Document 2: Modifies the project for flood damage reduction
authorize the Chief of Engineers to construct improvements to flood damage reduction facilities adjacent to those rivers
Document 3: Modifies the project for navigation, Atchafalaya River and Bayous
carry out projects for bank stabilization on the eastern shore of Crewboat Cut to prevent erosion

topic.name: Federal Government Efficiency Measures
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the efficiency and accountability of federal government operations.

Document 1: bars the Government Publishing Office from furnishing a printed copy of the Federal Register without charge
unless the Member or office requests a printed copy
Document 2: establishes a Government Reform Commission to examine the current configuration of executive agencies
review and report to the President and Congress on existing Government Accountability Office reports for reducing waste
Document 3: requires federal agency Chief Financial Officers to include multilingual services accounting information
requires the OMB Director's annual financial management status report to contain a Multilingual Services Expenses Report

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation and Safety
